questions.prompt: Which attribute holds the destination URL for a link?

explanations.overview: The href attribute on <a> defines where the link goes.

byOptionId.href: Correct — href on <a> specifies the link destination.
byOptionId.src: Incorrect — src is used for resources like images and scripts, not for anchor destinations.
byOptionId.link: Incorrect — 'link' is not an attribute for anchors.
byOptionId.url: Incorrect — 'url' is a generic term but not the anchor attribute name.

options.label: A link to another page on the same website
A link to a different domain
A link to a local file only
A link that only points to images

explanations.overview: Internal links point to pages within the same site.

byOptionId.same-site: Correct — internal links target pages on the same domain or site.
byOptionId.external: Incorrect — external links point to different domains.
byOptionId.file: Incorrect — linking to local files is possible but not the typical definition of internal links.
byOptionId.image: Incorrect — the target can be any resource, not specifically images.

options.label: Tells search engines not to follow the link
Opens the link in a new tab or window
Forces the browser to download the link

explanations.overview: target="_blank" opens the target in a new tab.

byOptionId.newtab: Correct — target="_blank" opens the destination in a new tab or window.
byOptionId.internal: Incorrect — target does not change whether a link is internal or external.
byOptionId.nofollow: Incorrect — rel="nofollow" is used for search engine hints, not target behavior.
byOptionId.download: Incorrect — the download attribute suggests downloading; target=_blank opens in a new tab.
